rationale: Automata methods like Dančík/Heineman rely on manually designed 'feasible triplets' or bounds. Deep RL can autonomously discover novel, tighter local bounding automata.
first_step: Define the state of an RL agent as a finite context window of the DP matrix, action as sequence emission, reward as LCS length. Train PPO.
expected_signal: The RL agent's average reward per step strictly exceeds 0.792665992, providing a constructible heuristic for a new formal lower bound.

rationale: LCS bounds can be rewritten as the zero-temperature free energy of a 2D statistical mechanics model. Tensor networks (DMRG) provide rigorous variational bounds on ground state energies.
first_step: Formulate the binary alphabet LCS as a local vertex model. Write a script to compute the exact transfer matrix for width N=10.
expected_signal: DMRG convergence to a leading eigenvalue whose log strictly bounds \gamma_2 from below, surpassing Lueker's bounds.

rationale: If the LCS maps precisely to the KPZ/TASEP class, exact formulas involving Fredholm determinants might yield the exact constant without simulating.
first_step: Investigate the exact map between Bukh & Cox's 'frog dynamics' (PushTASEP variant) and existing exact determinantal point process solutions.
expected_signal: An integral equation whose solution analytically matches or refines the 0.7926... / 0.8262... gap.
